statement.en: Effect of the abiotic environment on adaptation to moisture deficit in the grass Festuca ovina
statement.summary: The thesis aimed at understanding the effect of both biotic and abiotic factors on Festuca ovina and its adaptation to the edaphic microhabitats, in particular, to the stress of water deficit. The study focused on a wild population of F. ovina on the Baltic Island of Öland in southern Sweden where the diploid F. ovina populations were found to carry a second copy of cytosolic phosphoglucose isomerase gene (PgiC2). This second copy, PgiC2, was originally considered as a duplication of the native PgiC1 gene, but later proved to be natural transgene of acquired horizontally from Poa species. Therefore, we questioned whether the PgiC1 and PgiC2 are adaptive in the wild population of F. ovina. The high genetic diversity within the native and transgenic Pgi loci of the grass Festuca ovina might

reiTyping.dfumt8Reason: The document is dated 2026-04-25 (future date, likely data entry error) and marked as a thesis abstract. The core claims about horizontal gene transfer (HGT) of PgiC2 from Poa to Festuca ovina are empirically testable but the abstract is incomplete ('might' trailing). The adaptive significance claim requires experimental validation not provided in the excerpt. Status is provisional/pre-validated research.

ramanujanFormula.interpretation: 自己からの逃亡が、他者の贈り物によって水の戦いへと変わる。
